← all tutorials

CAP & PACELC — interactive CAP_TRADEOFFS.md cap_tradeoffs.py

Pick the CP or AP edge of the CAP triangle to see what each sacrifices. Then split the 5-node cluster and fire requests to watch CP error on the minority while AP returns data (possibly stale). The PACELC grid places real systems in the latency-vs-consistency quadrants. All responses are recomputed in JS from the identical model as cap_tradeoffs.py, and the green check: OK badge confirms they match.  |  deeper: split-brain & healing →

① The CAP triangle — during a partition you must drop C or A (P is unavoidable)

What to do: click CP (consistency-first) or AP (availability-first). The highlighted edge is what the system keeps; the dimmed corner is what it sacrifices when a partition strikes. P is always retained (networks fail).
5 nodes {A,B,C,D,E}, partition {A,B} | {C,D,E}, quorum = 3
🔗 This is the Gilbert & Lynch (2002) impossibility made concrete: you can keep at most two of {C, A, P}. Real networks always have P, so the live choice is C or A.

② Partition simulator — fire requests, watch CP error vs AP serve stale

What to do: cut the partition (the wall between {A,B} and {C,D,E}), pick CP or AP, then send a write 100 from the minority and a write 200 from the majority. CP errors on the minority side; AP accepts on both and diverges.
system:
Ready. Partition is ON; system is CP. Click a request button above.
quorum = floor(5/2)+1 = 3. Minority {A,B} = 2 < quorum; majority {C,D,E} = 3 ≥ quorum.

③ PACELC — the latency axis you pay even WITHOUT a partition

What to do: hover a quadrant to see its meaning. Partitioned → choose C or A (that's CAP). Else (healthy) → choose L (latency) or C (consistency). Click a system chip to see why it lives there.
Hover or click a quadrant / system chip.
PA/EL — AP family (Cassandra, DynamoDB, Riak) PC/EC — CP family (etcd, ZooKeeper, Spanner, HBase) PA/EC — fast under partition, strong when healthy (MongoDB configurable)
check: … JS CP/AP request model matches cap_tradeoffs.py GOLD CHECK (CP → ERROR on minority; AP → balance=100 on minority read).
